first_paragraph: **Costantino Lazzari** (1 January 1857 – 29 December 1927) was an Italian politician. He was one of the founders and main leaders of the [Italian Socialist Party](/wiki/Italian_Socialist_Party "Italian Socialist Party").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

**Costantino Lazzari** (1 January 1857 – 29 December 1927) was an Italian politician. He was one of the founders and main leaders of the [Italian Socialist Party](/wiki/Italian_Socialist_Party "Italian Socialist Party").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Costantino_Lazzari&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Constantino Lazzari was born in [Cremona](/wiki/Cremona "Cremona"), Italy, on 1 January 1857. He was an [artisan](/wiki/Artisan "Artisan") and since his adolescence, he was a member of the [left-wing](/wiki/Left-wing "Left-wing") [trade unions](/wiki/Trade_unions "Trade unions"). In 1882, Lazzari founded (with Giuseppe Croce) the [Italian Workers' Party](/wiki/Italian_Workers%27_Party "Italian Workers' Party") (POI).

In 1886, Lazzari commissioned [Filippo Turati](/wiki/Filippo_Turati "Filippo Turati") and [Amintore Galli](/wiki/Amintore_Galli "Amintore Galli") to compose *[Workers' Hymn](/wiki/Workers%27_Hymn "Workers' Hymn")*, considered among the most significant historic songs of the Italian workers' movement.[[2]](#cite_note-:11-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

In 1892, with Turati and [Anna Kuliscioff](/wiki/Anna_Kuliscioff "Anna Kuliscioff"), Lazzari founded the [Italian Socialist Party](/wiki/Italian_Socialist_Party "Italian Socialist Party") (PSI) at the [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa") Congress.

Lazzari was the leader of the [revolutionary](/wiki/Revolutionary_socialism "Revolutionary socialism") wing of the Socialist Party, known as *Massimalisti*. In 1912, he was elected Secretary of the Socialist Party and led the party in the [1913 general election](/wiki/1913_Italian_general_election "1913 Italian general election"), where the PSI gained 17.6% of votes, arriving second after the governing [Liberal Union](/wiki/Liberal_Union_(Italy) "Liberal Union (Italy)") of [Giovanni Giolitti](/wiki/Giovanni_Giolitti "Giovanni Giolitti"). In 1919, Lazzari resigned as Secretary.

In 1922, he was expelled from the PSI, accused of being a close ally of the newly-established [Communist Party](/wiki/Communist_Party_of_Italy "Communist Party of Italy") (PCdI), and for being in favour of the PSI joining the [Communist International](/wiki/Communist_International "Communist International").

After the formation of the [Fascist dictatorship](/wiki/Italian_Fascism "Italian Fascism") of [Benito Mussolini](/wiki/Benito_Mussolini "Benito Mussolini"), Lazzari was persecuted as a socialist and died in poverty in 1927.
